preamble: Denasalization and lowering of the nasalized close front vowel [ĩ] to the close-mid front vowel [e]. This shift involves loss of nasality combined with lowering of vowel height, occurring in Romance and Slavic languages through denasalization processes and vowel quality shifts in historical phonological change.

examples.note: In French, denasalization of nasal vowels combined with lowering of high nasal vowels toward mid vowel space, particularly in denasalization before voiceless obstruents.
In some Portuguese dialects, nasalized [ĩ] denasalizes and lowers to [e] in casual speech and before non-nasal consonants.
Proto-Slavic nasalized high vowels underwent denasalization with lowering, as seen in the development of Old Church Slavic and modern Polish nasal vowel systems.

related.label: Denasalization of mid front nasal vowel
